channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: The AI boom just found two new winners: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase
item.description: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan showed that Wall Street is a major beneficiary of the AI boom, with record revenue driven by surging trading and investment banking.

item.title: Here’s the inflation breakdown for June 2026 — in one chart
item.description: The consumer price index rose 3.5% in June from a year earlier, a deceleration after several months of upward moves.

item.title: Warsh pledges Fed policy 'regime change' to rid inflation 'tax' on American people
item.description: Warsh pledged Tuesday to "get monetary policy right" and defeat the inflation that has bedeviled the central bank for the past five years.

item.title: Stock futures are flat after soft inflation reading lifts market: Live updates
item.description: Stocks climbed Tuesday after a cooler-than-expected inflation report bolstered hopes that the Fed may not need to raise interest rates as aggressively.

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran before Hormuz Strait blockade restarts
item.description: President Donald Trump has said the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is over following multiple flare-ups of hostilities in the region.

item.title: Caution warranted on this red hot AI cloud data stock that's now 'priced for perfection,' says Todd Gordon
item.description: Datadog runs the control room for modern software: it pulls data from a company's servers, apps and cloud services into one place.

item.title: SK Hynix options begin trading. But another group of stocks is stealing its thunder
item.description: One explanation for the lack of notable call-buying is that the surge in single-stock ETFs and leveraged funds stole a big chunk of the speculative limelight.

item.title: Current and former employees sue Meta, alleging discrimination in using AI to conduct layoffs
item.description: The lawsuit filed by current and former Meta employees underscores rising concerns about AI's impact on jobs and people with disabilities in the workforce.

item.title: Yum's Taco Bell removes select items, says no confirmed link to U.S. cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Yum Brands' Taco Bell said on Tuesday it had removed limited items from some restaurants as a precaution but said U.S. health officials have not linked the widening outbreak of cyclosporiasis to the chain or any specific food product.

item.title: Elon Musk likely broke the law by giving voters $1 million, Wisconsin board says
item.description: Elon Musk likely broke Wisconsin law when he handed out $1 million checks to voters in the 2025 state Supreme Court election, a bipartisan panel found.

item.title: United Airlines' new upsell: Keeping other travelers out of the middle seat
item.description: United Airlines will allow customers to pay more to keep the middle seat open on its Airbus A321XLRs

item.title: Social Security cost-of-living adjustment estimate for 2027 falls as inflation cools
item.description: New estimates show Social Security's annual cost-of-living adjustment for 2027 may be between 3.7% and 3.8%. Here's what to know.

item.title: Jim Cramer says concerns about AI market froth are overblown. Here's why
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said today's stock market is far less concerning than it was during the dot-com bubble.

item.title: New York becomes first U.S. state to impose AI data center ban
item.description: New York became the first state to ban construction of 'hyperscale' AI data centers, for one year, after Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an executive order Tuesday.

item.title: Wednesday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
item.description: Stocks closed higher on Tuesday, helped by signs of easing inflation, which boosted investor hopes that the Federal Reserve can hold off on rate hikes for the moment.

item.title: Bipartisan group of senators propose Social Security reform process ahead of funding shortfall
item.description: Social Security's trust fund that helps pay retirement benefits may run out in about six years. A bipartisan group of senators is calling for earlier reform.

item.title: Lucid dismisses report that it is weighing filing for bankruptcy or going private after shares plunge
item.description: The report said Lucid was considering options that could include going private or filing for bankruptcy protection.

item.title: Barclays believes SK Hynix's U.S. shares can double. Here's why
item.description: The memory shortage will allow price increases that will boost revenue, Barclays says.

item.title: Zipline adds ex-Tesla, Uber, Waymo execs to make drone delivery mainstream across U.S.
item.description: Zipline is growing its drone delivery business in the U.S., and has hired former Tesla, Uber Eats and Waymo executives to help it scale up in new markets.

item.title: Oil prices rise as U.S. launches new Iran airstrikes, while Trump abandons Strait of Hormuz fee
item.description: Oil prices rose on Tuesday after U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans to impose shipping fees in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Johnson & Johnson has a chance to show it's more than just a rotation winner
item.description: The healthcare giant's second-quarter earnings will shine the spotlight on its exciting new products and pipeline.

item.title: Eyeing a credit card sign-up bonus? Here's the best time to apply
item.description: If you've been mulling over the idea of getting a new card for its sign-up bonus, here's a general timeline to consider.

item.title: Cybersecurity stocks rally on AI spending change comments from IBM's Krishna
item.description: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna told CNBC's Sara Eisen that some major deals were put on hold toward the end of the quarter as businesses rethink spending.

item.title: U.S. trade official says 'very few' Nvidia H200 AI chips have been shipped to China
item.description: The remark is a sign that H200 shipments to China have restarted, potentially boosting Nvidia's sales even higher.

item.title: IBM stock craters 25%, the worst day on record, after company issues second-quarter earnings warning
item.description: The CEO blamed the shortfall on weakness in the software and infrastructure business because clients shifted money toward hardware purchases.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: IBM, Goldman Sachs, CleanSpark, HCA Healthcare & more
item.description: These are some of the stocks posting the largest moves midday.

item.title: Supreme Court Justice Barrett says the threat level against judges 'is really high'
item.description: Supreme Court Justices Amy Coney Barrett and Elena Kagan are testifying about the high court's budget request, which includes higher spending for security.

item.title: T. rex sells for $50 million, becoming the most expensive dinosaur fossil ever auctioned
item.description: It passes a stegosaurus skeleton that billionaire hedge funder Ken Griffin bought for $44.6 million in 2024.

item.title: Warren Buffett is accelerating his charitable donations with aim to give away Berkshire wealth by 2034
item.description: Warren Buffett is speeding up the pace of his annual donations of Berkshire Hathaway shares to four family foundations, giving them a total of almost $6 billion now.

item.title: Apple in talks with startup that shrinks AI models to run on an iPhone
item.description: PrismML says its compressed version of Alibaba’s Qwen model uses up to 15 times less memory, potentially advancing Apple’s AI push.
